steps.title: Take the square root of both sides
steps.content:
Starting with $(x+7)^2=9$, take the square root of both sides. Remember the $\pm$:
$$x+7=\pm 3$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
From $x+7=3$: $x=-4$.
From $x+7=-3$: $x=-10$.
So the possible values are $-4$ and $-10$.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
Plug each into $(x+7)^2$ and see which gives 9:
  • A) $x=-2$: $(-2+7)^2=25 e 9$. Eliminate.
  • B) $x=2$: $(2+7)^2=81 e 9$. Eliminate.
  • C) $x=-3$: $(-3+7)^2=16 e 9$. Eliminate.
  • D) $x=-4$: $(-4+7)^2=9$ ✓.
D is correct.
